title: South China Sea dispute
context: An accelerating crisis over competing claims in the South China Sea (SCS) has brought the region close to armed confrontation. The waters carry roughly one-third of global maritime trade, hold about 10% of the annual global fisheries catch, and sit atop large untapped oil and gas reserves, making the disputed Spratly and Paracel islands, Scarborough Shoal, and Macclesfield Bank strategically vital. China asserts "indisputable sovereignty" over most of the sea based on its ten-dashed-line map and has conducted exten

parties.redLine: No concession on sovereignty over Xisha, Nansha, or any part of the ten-dashed-line claim.

parties.name: United States

parties.redLine: No acceptance of the ten-dashed-line or any historic-rights claim exceeding UNCLOS-provided maritime zones.

parties.name: Philippines
parties.endState: International recognition of Philippine territorial rights in the WPS, achieved through a binding UNCLOS-based settlement and a binding, enforceable WPS Code of Conduct.
parties.redLine: No recognition or legitimization of China's nine/ten-dashed line claim.

parties.name: Vietnam
parties.endState: Peaceful, law-based resolution of East Sea claims under UNCLOS, with China accepting negotiation or arbitration, backed by a strong ASEAN and improved US/regional-power engagement
parties.redLine: No bilateral-only settlement with China on terms favorable to Beijing; overlapping claims must go to multilateral negotiation or UNCLOS arbitration.

parties.name: Malaysia
parties.endState: Peaceful multilateral resolution of SCS sovereignty disputes grounded in UNCLOS, with increased cooperation and joint commercial ventures between ASEAN states and China.
parties.redLine: No recognition of the Chinese ten/nine-dashed line or any historic-rights claim exceeding UNCLOS.

parties.redLine: China's ten-dashed-line boundary is unacceptable and cannot be legitimized.

parties.redLine: China's ten-dashed-line claim is illegitimate under the PCA award and unacceptable.

parties.name: India
parties.endState: Peaceful, UNCLOS-based resolution of SCS sovereignty disputes with reduced Chinese naval presence, preserved freedom of navigation, and Chinese respect for Indian regional interest
parties.redLine: Will walk out of negotiations if the India-China border dispute (Jammu-Kashmir, common border) is raised; talks are strictly about SCS.
